concepts.label: Autonomous vehicles
concepts.definition: Driverless cars and shuttles whose promise is constrained by mapped domains, edge cases, road space, and sprawl.

concepts.label: Robotaxi services
concepts.definition: Driverless taxi services running in a few cities but limited by narrow, heavily mapped domains.

concepts.label: Autonomous shuttles
concepts.definition: Fixed-route autonomous vehicles predicted to scale before general robotaxis.

concepts.label: Edge cases
concepts.definition: Rare, weird street interactions that dominate autonomy safety risk.

concepts.label: Miles-per-intervention
concepts.definition: An autonomy metric called misleading because rare tail events dominate risk.

concepts.label: Deployment speed
concepts.definition: A criterion that weights benefits by how quickly an intervention can be delivered.
members: benefit per year of deployment time

concepts.label: Street data
concepts.definition: Data that enables dynamic curb pricing, coordinated mobility stacks, and reprogrammable streets.

concepts.label: E-bikes
concepts.definition: Low-cost electric bikes with little parking footprint that work well in dense corridors and short trips.

concepts.label: Shared e-scooters
concepts.definition: Shared micromobility vehicles whose economics and climate value depend on durability and trip context.

concepts.label: Protected bike lanes
concepts.definition: Protected cycling lanes named as a boring but high-impact intervention.

concepts.label: Cargo bikes and micro-hubs
concepts.definition: A potential underexplored answer for exploding urban logistics.

concepts.label: Short urban car trips
concepts.definition: Mostly sub-5 km urban car trips that e-bikes and transit can address.

concepts.label: Walking replacement risk
concepts.definition: The concern that scooters may replace walking rather than car trips, weakening climate benefits.

concepts.label: Transit
concepts.definition: A capacity-focused mobility mode that can be extended by scooters and strengthened by investment.

concepts.label: Dedicated bus lanes
concepts.definition: Street lanes reserved for buses that improve transit and can deploy faster than rail.

concepts.label: Bus rapid transit
concepts.definition: A bus system using dedicated lanes and signal priority to get much of light rail's benefit faster and cheaper.

concepts.label: First and last mile transit
concepts.definition: Connections to transit stops that extend transit catchment and can pull trips off cars.

concepts.label: Geometry problem
concepts.definition: The spatial inefficiency of moving people in single-occupant boxes regardless of driver.

concepts.label: Spatial efficiency
concepts.definition: Moving more people through the same street space, favoring transit, bikes, and walking over cars.

concepts.label: Curb management
concepts.definition: Governance of contested curb space used by delivery, ride-hail, scooters, buses, and parking.

concepts.label: Reprogrammable street space
concepts.definition: Streets treated as instrumented, re-allocatable, and priced rather than fixed concrete.

concepts.label: Reduced car dependence
concepts.definition: The author's chosen north star metric for judging mobility solutions.

concepts.label: Congestion pricing
concepts.definition: A road-pricing policy described as producing fast, durable traffic reductions.

concepts.label: Pricing and allocation
concepts.definition: The final framing that scarce street space should be priced and allocated rather than solved by vehicles.
members: price and allocate scarce street space

concepts.label: Induced demand
concepts.definition: The tendency for added road capacity to fill with new traffic.

concepts.label: Evaporating demand
concepts.definition: The effect where reducing car lanes can reduce total car traffic rather than merely displacing it.

concepts.label: Dynamic curb pricing
concepts.definition: Pricing curb use dynamically, predicted to emerge in major cities within a decade.

concepts.label: 15-minute city
concepts.definition: A land-use model where daily needs are within a short walk or ride.

concepts.label: Zoning reform
concepts.definition: Changing zoning is implied as needed because past zoning separated jobs and housing.

concepts.label: Jobs-housing co-location
concepts.definition: Placing jobs and housing near each other, an assumption of the 15-minute city.

concepts.label: Context-dependent mode shift
concepts.definition: The idea that scooter substitution differs by setting, replacing cars near transit and walking in walkable cores.

concepts.label: Charging infrastructure
concepts.definition: The hidden electrification bottleneck around where and how fast vehicles can plug in.

concepts.label: Curbside charging
concepts.definition: Dense-neighborhood vehicle charging at the curb, described as basically unsolved.

concepts.label: Swappable batteries
concepts.definition: Battery systems in newer scooter fleets that help make payback periods more viable.

concepts.label: Vehicle-to-grid
concepts.definition: Using parked EVs as distributed storage, blocked by missing standards and incentives.

concepts.label: Urban mobility
concepts.definition: A field spanning transit, micromobility, autonomy, and city policy.

concepts.label: Mobility layer fragmentation
concepts.definition: The risk that each actor optimizes its own layer instead of the whole mobility system.

concepts.label: Mobility stack
concepts.definition: A strategy combining demand pricing, transit and bikes, constrained autonomy, and better curb and street data.

concepts.label: Urban logistics
concepts.definition: The freight and delivery side of mobility that the author identifies for future study.

concepts.label: Equity
concepts.definition: The concern that pricing and premium mobility can entrench access gaps without safeguards.
